name: Almond-Orange Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a baking sheet.
Shake off any excess fat or crumbs.
2. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients. Add the eggs, vegetable oil,
almond extract, orange zest, chopped almonds, and vegetable shortening to the well.
Mix with your hands until a smooth dough forms. Divide the dough into two equal parts.
3. On a lightly floured surface, shape each portion of dough into 2 flat-bottomed
cylinders, about 1 inch high, 2 1/2 inches wide, and 8 inches long.
Place the shaped dough on the prepared baking sheet with cut sides down.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, until lightly golden on top. Remove from oven and let cool slightly.
4. Using a sharp knife held by the handle and tip, cut each cylinder into
diagonal slices of about 3/4 inch (1.9 cm) thick. Place the slices back onto the baking sheet,
with the cut sides down. Return to the oven for an additional 15 minutes, until the edges are golden brown
and the biscotti have dried slightly.
5. Remove from oven and cool completely on wire racks. Store in an airtight container.
Optional: For added flavor and texture, dip one end of each slice into melted chocolate before cooling.
